CREATE TABLE test_list (id INTEGER, items LIST<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO test_list VALUES (1, [1, 1, NULL, -3, 1, 5]), (2, [NULL, NULL, NULL]), (3, [2, 2, 2, 2]), (4, []);
SELECT id, array_distinct(items) AS distinct_items FROM test_list WHERE items IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, array_distinct(array_concat(items, [7, 5, 6])) AS unique_combined_items FROM test_list;
CREATE TABLE test_data (user_id INTEGER, preferences LIST<INTEGER>);
SELECT user_id, array_distinct(preferences) AS distinct_preferences FROM test_data;
SELECT t1.id, array_distinct(array_concat(t1.items, t2.preferences)) AS merged_distinct FROM test_list t1 JOIN test_data t2 ON t1.id = t2.user_id;
SELECT array_distinct(array_map(x -> x * 2, items)) FROM test_list WHERE id = 1;
SELECT array_distinct(array_filter(items, x -> x IS NOT NULL)) FROM test_list WHERE id = 3;
SELECT id, array_distinct(items) AS no_duplicates FROM test_list WHERE array_length(items) > 0;
